mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals imminent policy easing and lower discount rates, which raises equity present values and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is also decelerating, because the market can price cuts without fearing a renewed inflation shock.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's sign flips when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates a weakening labor market and slower future demand, which compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should activate only when unemployment rises sharply enough to suggest recession risk rather than normal volatility.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS reflects stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which improves earnings revisions and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is stronger when household demand is not offset by a rising unemployment rate.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth fails to predict above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and softer real-side momentum, which tends to precede equity drawdowns as margins and earnings expectations are revised lower. The signal is most informative when production falls below its prior three-month average.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX forward returns are not negative on average after INDPRO downside breaks, or if the trigger does not improve hit rate versus a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below recent trend signals weakening housing demand and broader cyclical softening, which reduces risk appetite and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is short-horizon because housing is a leading indicator for near-term sentiment rather than immediate earnings.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns are not negative after large HOUST drops, or if the signal is indistinguishable from noise in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated, the market interprets policy as restrictive and growth-hostile, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. This is a threshold regime: high rates matter only when inflation prevents an easy pivot toward cuts.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the conditional 20-day return after high-rate, high-inflation observations is not below zero, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly GDP surprise in GDPC1 should support equity prices over a full quarter because stronger real activity raises expected corporate cash flows. The effect is strongest in broad recoveries and fails when the GDP move is revised away before the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not higher following positive GDPC1 growth surprises, or if the relationship disappears after accounting for revision-heavy quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE indicates labor-market breadth rather than cyclical overheating, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if payroll gains are accompanied by a rising unemployment rate, which would imply hidden labor weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if months with payroll gains and flat/falling UNRATE do not outperform baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the advantage disappears when UNRATE rises alongside PAYEMS.
